Use this skill when implementing a feature or fixing a bug test-first.

## Concepts (timeless)
- Red ‚Üí Green ‚Üí Refactor: write a failing test that pins the behavior, make it pass minimally, then improve with the test as a safety net.
- Test behavior through the public surface, not private internals ‚Äî tests should survive refactors.
- One reason to fail per test; arrange/act/assert; deterministic (no time/network/order coupling).
- Cover the unit, the integration boundary, and the critical end-to-end path ‚Äî not just happy-path units.
- A bug fix starts with a test that reproduces the bug.

## Pull current docs first
Test runners, assertion libs, and mocking tools differ per stack ‚Äî check the current docs:
- your test runner + assertion library's current docs

## Checklist
- [ ] A failing test exists before the implementation
- [ ] Tests assert observable behavior via the public API
- [ ] Each test is deterministic and isolated
- [ ] Edge cases + error paths covered, not only happy path
- [ ] Bug fixes include a regression test
